visa_types.name: SHORT STAY - BUSINESS VISA
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/ita/travel-insurance)
All of documents must be translated in English or in Italian.
The document required below is applicable for applicant submitted in Danang and Ho Chi Minh city. If you apply in Danang, All the translations in English or Italian of the Vietnamese documents shall be done by Justice Committee Division in Danang.
The applicant must present the original and a true copy of each document at VFS for your visa application submission. All original documents must be notarized copies within the last 6 months from the time of submission. Notarization is required to be performed by an authorized third party rather than by the issuing authority of the document.
To facilitate the submission process, please kindly arrange your papers into the following order:
- Checklist for Business Visa (Download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4gKTRYLVFpK0DkxH27Xnht/51675ebc4bb7eafa0eb78cb5c9e010e5/checklist-business-2024.pdf)). The applicant needs to print out this checklist and sign to submit along with the visa application.
- Visa Application Form (accurately fulfilled and signed by the applicant) (Download  here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4k5TGK1Vbp8WTJX5FqE7lF/d83190cfa94981ec2489b3d76f059879/application-form-new-update-hcm.pdf))
- 2 photos of the applicant must be taken within the last six (6) months (4x6cm or 3.5x4.5cm, white background)
- Original passport valid for at least three months after visa expiry  date containing at least 2 blank pages
- Copy of Flight Booking (s). This booking needs to be sent directly from the airline company with contacts and logo and provide all the necessary information of the flight(s). In case of traveling to more than 2 countries in Schengen, you will be required to submit your itinerary and proof of transportation for the ENTIRE period in Schengen area, such as flight booking, train or coach booking, etc… Name of applicant(s) need to be shown on all booking (s).
- A copy of Travel Insurance, valid for all countries in the Schengen States, for the whole period of stay with minimum coverage is more than 30.000 Euro for each category (medical expenses and repatriation).
- For duration of stay in Schengen States less than 5 days, you will be required to acquire travel insurance valid for a minimum period of 7 days. For example, you intend to stay in Schengen States from 20 January to 23 January 2015 (4 days), and then you need to buy a travel insurance validated from 20 January to 26 January 2015 (7 days).
- For duration of stay in Schengen States from 6 days to more, you will be required to buy a travel insurance validated in the entire duration of stay plus to 2 extra days. For example, if you intend to stay in Schengen States from 20 January to 26 January 2015 (7 days), then you need to buy a travel insurance validated from 20 to 28 January 2015 (9 days).
Note: If you buy travel insurance with coverage of 30,000 Euro, the insurance agency should confirm minimum coverage is more than 30,000 Euro; if they state that maximum coverage is 30,000 Euro, you need to require the insurance agency to correct it as above request; otherwise, you must upgrade your insurance with higher coverage than 30,000 Euro.
- Copy of proof of accommodation during the ENTIRE stay in Schengen area
- Hotel booking (s). Name of the applicant needs to be shown on the hotel booking (s). The date on flight booking and hotel booking must be logically matched. For example, if you intend to depart on 20 January 2015, and arrive in Italy on 21 January 2015, and you will leave Italy on 28 January 2015, in this case, your hotel booking in Italy must be checked in on 21 January 2015 and checked out on 28 January 2015. We will not accept a hotel booking with the check-in date and check-out date earlier or later than your departure and arrival, even if the difference is only one day. In case applicants book hotel(s) on airbnb.com, the hotel(s) where you reserved accommodation must have to send booking confirmation(s) directly to Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City via email address hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it)
- In case of staying in a private accommodation in Italy or in any other Member State(s), a fulfilled “Proof of Sponsorship and/or private accommodation” form (download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/63HjYdZ8de5uiAtFcHeSkQ/bea41cbbd511807e448d9597f4f1a543/lettera-di-invito-per-turismo-eu-gdpr-compliant.pdf)). The inviting person needs to send a scanned invitation which bears the inviter’s signature, accompanied with the photocopy of his/her passport/identity card and permesso di soggiorno (if the inviting does not hold Italian/European citizenship) to the Consulate in Ho Chi Minh City via hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it) or fax +84 8 38275444.
- The applicant will present a hard copy of the above-mentioned papers upon submission at VFS.
- Documents from the applicant’s company in Viet Nam:
- A notarized copy of valid labour contract
- Original pay slips of last 3 months
- Original decision on sending staff to travel to Italy
- Business Registration/ Investment License of the applicant’s employer. Name of the company on the business registration/ investment license must be matched with name of the company on the valid labour contract. Noted: A notarized copy in Vietnamese of Business Registration/ Investment License must be certified and legalized at Ho Chi Minh Department for Foreign Affairs (Address: 184bis Pasteur, District 1, HCMC). We will not accept a Business License copy which has been certified at any other Department for Foreign Affairs.
- A notarized copy of TAX/ Payment to State Budget (last 3 months/ the last quarters). Attention: if the company pays tax at the bank, the copies of the receipts issued by the bank must be notarized by a public notary office. In case the copies cannot be notarized, the copies of the receipts need to be stamped on with the company’s seal, and applicants are requested to present the original receipts on the date of submission. In case company pays tax by online bank transfer, the electronic tax declaration sheets also need to be stamped on with the company’s seal.
- A bank statement of the company’s account (last 3 months up to the date of application, with the original seal from the bank) OR a verification letter confirming the present balance of the company’s account (with the original seal from the bank) is required.
- In case the Vietnamese company is attending a fair/ exhibition in Italy: display plan, display photo etc
- *In cases where a third company covers the applicant’s expenses: 
- Original Letter of Financial Sponsorship from the sponsor, clearly stating the commitment to cover all related expenses.
- Documentary evidence of the relationship between the applicant and the sponsor, such as a cooperation/partnership agreement, commercial contracts, invoices, or other equivalent documents duly notarized.
- Legal and financial documents of the sponsoring company/organization, (This set of documents is applicable only if the sponsoring party is a private enterprise) including:
- Business Registration/ Investment License. Noted: A notarized copy in Vietnamese of Business Registration/ Investment License must be certified and legalized at Ho Chi Minh Department for Foreign Affairs (Address: 184bis Pasteur, District 1, HCMC).
- A notarized copy of TAX/ Payment to State Budget (last 3 months/ the last quarter). Attention: if the company pays tax at the bank, the copies of the receipts issued by the bank must be notarized by a public notary office. In case the copies cannot be notarized, the copies of the receipts need to be stamped on with the company’s seal, and applicants are requested to present the original receipts on the date of submission. In case company pays tax by online bank transfer, the electronic tax declaration sheets also need to be stamped on with the company’s seal.
- A bank statement of the company’s account (last 3 months up to the date of application, with the original seal from the bank) OR a verification letter confirming the present balance of the company’s account (with the original seal from the bank) is required.
- Documents from the company in Italy:
- Letter of invitation from the  Italian company. Noted: the letter of invitation must follow the official  format of the Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City; download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6omKXzotjmLUULBv4MYpT/c9838f65e17bb783f7176a023894f648/modello-di-invito-per-affari-eu-gdpr-compliant.pdf)
- A copy of travel agenda  indicating schedules business contacts.
- A copy of valid ID card or  passport of the inviter from the Italian company.
- A copy of Business Registration (Visura Camerale) of the Italian company.
- All the above listed documents must be sent directly from the Italian company to the Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City via email hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it) and hochiminh.commerciale@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.commerciale@esteri.it).  The applicant also needs to bring these documents in paper along with the visa application at VFS centre.
- Proof of financial status:
- Last 3 months bank statement. (Original one with seal from the bank). The bank statement should be created maximum only 1 or 2 days before the date when you submit your application at VFS Centre. For example, if you intend to submit your visa application on 20 January 2015, you will be advised to submit your bank statement from  18 October 2014 until 18 or 20 January 2015. In case your monthly salary is transferred via banking, you will be required to submit the salary bank statement. Business owner with the percentage of capital contribution under 100% cannot submit the bank statement of his company. For your convenience, please, consider the “Table of economic subsistence for the duration of stay (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/vietnam/english/pdf/subsistence-table-for-duration-of-stay-ENG.pdf)”, the table does not take into account hotel expenses and travel tickets. 
- Notarized  copy of bank saving book/ Bank Account Verification ( if any and only if opened one year before the submission date)

## Overview

          If you plan to go to Italy on a trip for business visit or short professional training for a period not to exceed 90 days and your country is not on the visa-free list, you need a Schengen travel visa (Visa C). This visa is not a work permit.
Kindly note that the period from the date of submission of visa application at VFS center until the intended arrival date in Schengen area must not exceed 6 months.

## Visa Fees

[ita > vnm > en > Table:SHORT STAY - BUSINESS VISA > Visa Fees 1]
- The visa listed above are subject to change from time to time based on the exchange rate determined by the Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City and the Embassy of Italy in Ha Noi.
- In addition to the visa fees, VFS Global will apply a surcharge of 245.000 VND (including VAT) is applied for each visa application presented at the VFS Center.
- For       more convenient, the applicant can choose a “special courier service” for       the return of the passport for an additional cost of 134,000 VND (including       VAT). 
- All payments must be made in local currency (VND), either in cash or by credit/debit card at the submission counter, and are not refundable in case of visa refusal. (Please note that VFS Italy in Hanoi only accepts payment by cash, VFS Italy in Ho Chi Minh and Danang accept cash, credit/ debit card payment).
- The  visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following  categories:
- children under six years;
- all kind of study visa. ( Special cases with permission of the Italian Embassy and the Consulate General)
- The visa fee may be waived for (Special cases with permission of the Italian Embassy and the Consulate General):
- children from the age of six years and below the age of 12 years;
- holder of diplomatic and service passports
- Spouse  and child of citizen of EU or EEA.
- Parents - in - law of citizen of EU
- For applicants’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support applicants in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/ita/premium-services)”

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/ita/travel-insurance)
All of documents must be translated in English or in Italian.
The document required below is applicable for applicant submitted in Danang and Ho Chi Minh city. If you apply in Danang, All the translations in English or Italian of the Vietnamese documents shall be done by Justice Committee Division in Danang.
The applicant must present the original and a true copy of each document at VFS for your visa application submission. All original documents must be notarized copies within the last 6 months from the time of submission. Notarization is required to be performed by an authorized third party rather than by the issuing authority of the document.
To facilitate the submission process, please kindly arrange your papers into the following order:
- Checklist for Business Visa (Download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4gKTRYLVFpK0DkxH27Xnht/51675ebc4bb7eafa0eb78cb5c9e010e5/checklist-business-2024.pdf)). The applicant needs to print out this checklist and sign to submit along with the visa application.
- Visa Application Form (accurately fulfilled and signed by the applicant) (Download  here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4k5TGK1Vbp8WTJX5FqE7lF/d83190cfa94981ec2489b3d76f059879/application-form-new-update-hcm.pdf))
- 2 photos of the applicant must be taken within the last six (6) months (4x6cm or 3.5x4.5cm, white background)
- Original passport valid for at least three months after visa expiry  date containing at least 2 blank pages
- Copy of Flight Booking (s). This booking needs to be sent directly from the airline company with contacts and logo and provide all the necessary information of the flight(s). In case of traveling to more than 2 countries in Schengen, you will be required to submit your itinerary and proof of transportation for the ENTIRE period in Schengen area, such as flight booking, train or coach booking, etc… Name of applicant(s) need to be shown on all booking (s).
- A copy of Travel Insurance, valid for all countries in the Schengen States, for the whole period of stay with minimum coverage is more than 30.000 Euro for each category (medical expenses and repatriation).
- For duration of stay in Schengen States less than 5 days, you will be required to acquire travel insurance valid for a minimum period of 7 days. For example, you intend to stay in Schengen States from 20 January to 23 January 2015 (4 days), and then you need to buy a travel insurance validated from 20 January to 26 January 2015 (7 days).
- For duration of stay in Schengen States from 6 days to more, you will be required to buy a travel insurance validated in the entire duration of stay plus to 2 extra days. For example, if you intend to stay in Schengen States from 20 January to 26 January 2015 (7 days), then you need to buy a travel insurance validated from 20 to 28 January 2015 (9 days).
Note: If you buy travel insurance with coverage of 30,000 Euro, the insurance agency should confirm minimum coverage is more than 30,000 Euro; if they state that maximum coverage is 30,000 Euro, you need to require the insurance agency to correct it as above request; otherwise, you must upgrade your insurance with higher coverage than 30,000 Euro.
- Copy of proof of accommodation during the ENTIRE stay in Schengen area
- Hotel booking (s). Name of the applicant needs to be shown on the hotel booking (s). The date on flight booking and hotel booking must be logically matched. For example, if you intend to depart on 20 January 2015, and arrive in Italy on 21 January 2015, and you will leave Italy on 28 January 2015, in this case, your hotel booking in Italy must be checked in on 21 January 2015 and checked out on 28 January 2015. We will not accept a hotel booking with the check-in date and check-out date earlier or later than your departure and arrival, even if the difference is only one day. In case applicants book hotel(s) on airbnb.com, the hotel(s) where you reserved accommodation must have to send booking confirmation(s) directly to Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City via email address hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it)
- In case of staying in a private accommodation in Italy or in any other Member State(s), a fulfilled “Proof of Sponsorship and/or private accommodation” form (download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/63HjYdZ8de5uiAtFcHeSkQ/bea41cbbd511807e448d9597f4f1a543/lettera-di-invito-per-turismo-eu-gdpr-compliant.pdf)). The inviting person needs to send a scanned invitation which bears the inviter’s signature, accompanied with the photocopy of his/her passport/identity card and permesso di soggiorno (if the inviting does not hold Italian/European citizenship) to the Consulate in Ho Chi Minh City via hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it) or fax +84 8 38275444.
- The applicant will present a hard copy of the above-mentioned papers upon submission at VFS.

- A notarized copy of valid labour contract
- Original pay slips of last 3 months
- Original decision on sending staff to travel to Italy
- Business Registration/ Investment License of the applicant’s employer. Attention: if the company pays tax at the bank, the copies of the receipts issued by the bank must be notarized by a public notary office. In case company pays tax by online bank transfer, the electronic tax declaration sheets also need to be stamped on with the company’s seal.
- A bank statement of the company’s account (last 3 months up to the date of application, with the original seal from the bank) OR a verification letter confirming the present balance of the company’s account (with the original seal from the bank) is required.
- In case the Vietnamese company is attending a fair/ exhibition in Italy: display plan, display photo etc
- *In cases where a third company covers the applicant’s expenses: 
- Original Letter of Financial Sponsorship from the sponsor, clearly stating the commitment to cover all related expenses.
- Documentary evidence of the relationship between the applicant and the sponsor, such as a cooperation/partnership agreement, commercial contracts, invoices, or other equivalent documents duly notarized.
- Legal and financial documents of the sponsoring company/organization, (This set of documents is applicable only if the sponsoring party is a private enterprise) including:

In case the copies cannot be notarized, the copies of the receipts need to be stamped on with the company’s seal, and applicants are requested to present the original receipts on the date of submission. In case company pays tax by online bank transfer, the electronic tax declaration sheets also need to be stamped on with the company’s seal.
- A bank statement of the company’s account (last 3 months up to the date of application, with the original seal from the bank) OR a verification letter confirming the present balance of the company’s account (with the original seal from the bank) is required.
- Documents from the company in Italy:
- Letter of invitation from the  Italian company. Noted: the letter of invitation must follow the official  format of the Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City; download here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6omKXzotjmLUULBv4MYpT/c9838f65e17bb783f7176a023894f648/modello-di-invito-per-affari-eu-gdpr-compliant.pdf)
- A copy of travel agenda  indicating schedules business contacts.
- A copy of valid ID card or  passport of the inviter from the Italian company.
- A copy of Business Registration (Visura Camerale) of the Italian company.
- All the above listed documents must be sent directly from the Italian company to the Consulate General of Italy in Ho Chi Minh City via email hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it) and hochiminh.commerciale@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.commerciale@esteri.it).  The applicant also needs to bring these documents in paper along with the visa application at VFS centre.
- Proof of financial status:
- Last 3 months bank statement. (Original one with seal from the bank). The bank statement should be created maximum only 1 or 2 days before the date when you submit your application at VFS Centre. For example, if you intend to submit your visa application on 20 January 2015, you will be advised to submit your bank statement from  18 October 2014 until 18 or 20 January 2015. In case your monthly salary is transferred via banking, you will be required to submit the salary bank statement. Business owner with the percentage of capital contribution under 100% cannot submit the bank statement of his company. For your convenience, please, consider the “Table of economic subsistence for the duration of stay (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/vietnam/english/pdf/subsistence-table-for-duration-of-stay-ENG.pdf)”, the table does not take into account hotel expenses and travel tickets. 
- Notarized  copy of bank saving book/ Bank Account Verification ( if any and only if opened one year before the submission date)
- Notarized copy of certification of shareholder ship ( if any)

- All fees would be collected in cash at the application submission counter.

## Documents Required
LONG STAY VISA (Type D). The request of any type of National Visa together with proper documentation must be submitted in person only at the Italy Visa Application Centre in Hochiminh and Danang based on the territorial regions under the jurisdiction of the Italy Consulate.
All of documents must be translated in English or in Italian.
If you apply in mentioned Centers, please kindly read carefully this guideline and to prepare the checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Iz76okKKlsQGufEcz02ck/c4e0302136ee44f5d72c13ba7acf8a99/checklist-for-subordinate-employment-hochiminh.pdf) as attached.
Please note that the applicant has to send their " NULLA OSTA (AUTHORATION) of the relevant SUI issued no later than 6 months" to the Consulate's email hochiminh.visti@esteri.it (mailto:hochiminh.visti@esteri.it) before they submit their documents in the Center.
For applicants having the previous Schengen visa within the last 59 months, applicant is required to be present and to provide their fingerprints at VFS. For minor applicant, it is mandatory to present at the VFS office at the moment of submission.
